summary: Fix the cross-section residuals after the Block N application (stale names, T2=3-4→4, §2 schema copies)

names.notes:
All active-prose character references swept to canonical V2.2 names: Munenori->Shigemasa, Jūbei->Kihei, Ranpo->Sōan. 24 active uses fixed across §1.8 personae descriptions were already correct; the climb/spine granter tables (T0 R-rungs, T1 V-rungs, T2 G-rungs), the cold-open T0 beats, the milestone/M-plan prose, and the §2.15 SEEDS list were the residuals.

EXCEPTIONS LEFT INTACT (deliberate real-name/rename references — per task spec): L714/720/724 (the persona '(Renamed off the Yagyū Munenori / Jūbei / Edogawa Ranpo echo)' notes); L1028-1030 (the rename-explanation paragraph mapping old->new); L4924-4925 (the §6.6 rename mapping/denylist line 'Munenori->Shigemasa, Jūbei->Kihei, Ranpo->Sōan'); L5873-5874 (the 'off the Yagyū Munenori/Jūbei echo, off the Edogawa Ranpo echo' verifier-tie-out explanation).

ONE BORDERLINE LEFT AS-IS — FLAGGING FOR HUMAN: L6339 lists 'Tōkichi, Yagōemon, Jūbei, gōshi, rōnin, kyō-masu' as examples of name/display strings that must carry Hepburn macrons for the no-plain-ASCII-romaji lint. 'Jūbei' here is a typographic macron EXAMPLE, not a story/mechanics character reference, so it falls outside the rename scope; also 'Kihei' has no macron and could not serve as a macron example. I left it unchanged, but since Jūbei is no longer a canon character the human may want to swap the example for another macron-bearing canon name (the list already includes Tōkichi/Yagōemon; Sōan would also work). Did not touch it to stay surgical/in-scope.

fixes: Fix 1 (T2 revealed-pillar count 3–4 → 4): replaced **T2 = 3–4** → **T2 = 4** in 5 places (lines ~563, 2094, 2651, 6982, 7382 via replace_all); 'surfacing → 3–4)' → 'surfacing → 4)' in 2 table rows (lines ~582, 2107 via replace_all); 'the revealed-pillar set reaches **3–4**' → '**4**' (line ~341); 'T2 = + Name** (3–4).' → '(4).' (line ~635); 'now over **3–4 revealed pillars** (Arms + Estate + Office...)' → '**4 revealed pillars**' (line ~2995); 'pillars** (3–4 — Estate + Office + Arms...)' → '(4 —' (line ~5375); 'good in the 3–4 revealed pillars + great in 2–3 + excellent in 1–2' → 'good in the 4 revealed pillars...' (line ~7391); 'revealed-pillar set T2=3–4) all play' → 'T2=4)' (line ~7408).

Fix 2a (§2.2 appraisal state boolean → counter): line ~1256 SeasonalAppraisalState definition changed `pendingAppraisalDue` → `pendingAppraisals: number`, reconciling §2.2 with the §6.4 data model (counter drained in a loop, B10).

Fix 2b (§7 roadmap lunar de-bundling, D-Q6): line ~7061 'deriveDayKeyed(seed, \'weather\', day) helper (weather/lunar/festival derived-on-read, NOT stored)' reworded to '(weather/festival (day-keyed) + lunar (a real ~29.5-day ephemeris), derived-on-read, NOT stored)' so lunar is no longer bundled with the day-keyed RNG weather.
pillarsSchema.notes:
File edited: /Users/raynos/projects/games/kami-kakushi/docs/prd.md (only file touched). All edits surgical via Edit tool; voice/structure/links preserved.

Deliberately LEFT UNCHANGED — two residual '3–4' occurrences at lines ~6273 and ~6387 are meta-references that read "...NOT '3–4'" / "a single value **4** ... NOT '3–4'" — they document the V2.2 decision itself (B13/D-Q3), so editing them would corrupt the explanation. The original grep surfaced no unrelated '3–4 weapons/min/rungs' ranges, so none were at risk.

Post-edit grep for '3–4'/'3-4' confirms only the two intentional meta-references remain. The §6.4 data model (lines ~6132, ~6200–6201, ~6401) already used `pendingAppraisals: number`, so §2.2 now matches it (newest-wins reconciliation, no §6 change needed). Other lunar mentions (e.g. §6.7 line ~6818 'a real continuous ~29.5-day ephemeris') already distinguished lunar from a seed-keyed roll and needed no change; only the §7 roadmap foundation milestone bundled them.

fixes: docs/prd.md line ~1785 (§2.12 (a)): changed the one-line quest gloss from "**Quests** = sequential tasks advanced by game events." to "**Quests** = an **order-free SET of advance-events** (no fixed order, no `step` cursor; D-Q-B14) advanced by game events." — retiring the sequential-step framing.

docs/prd.md line 1818 (§2.12 (c) Rough DATA shape): retired the orphan `QuestTask { advancedBy (gameEvent), optionalDiscoveryNodes[] }` bullet and replaced it with the §6.4 runtime model: `{ status: QuestStatus; advancedBy: Set<QuestEventId> }` with `QuestStatus = 'taken' | 'active' | 'abandoned' | 'done' | 'failed'`, an UNORDERED SET of advance-events with NO `step` cursor, completing once required `advanceEvents[]` are all in the set in any order (D-Q-B14); kept the `optionalDiscoveryNodes[]` "discovery, not waypoints" note by attaching it per-event.
quests.notes:
Two surgical edits to /Users/raynos/projects/games/kami-kakushi/docs/prd.md, both in §2.12 (Dialogue & quests). They reconcile the §2 prose with the §6.4 order-free model (`{ status: QuestStatus; advancedBy: Set<QuestEventId> }`) and D-Q-B14.

Already consistent, left untouched: (1) the `maxAwards`/`repeatable` repeatable-deed field and the `advanceEvents[]` field on the `Quest` data shape (lines 1811-1817) — these already match the §6.5 content/quests.ts model. (2) The §2 `gatesSpine: false` field (line 1812 for Quest, line 1866 for Rumour) — these are inert set fields consistent with the §6.6 note that `gatesSpine` is always-false in v1 with no consumer (verifier asserts false); they are NOT framed as a live gating consumer, so no change was warranted. (3) Line 1787 already said "never an A→B→C waypoint list".

File-wide grep confirmed the only residual orphans of the sequential/step/task model in §2 were the two lines I fixed; the §6 region (lines 6088, 6197-6198, 6298, 6390-6391, 6514, 7251) already carries the order-free model and was out of scope.
